summary: AI agents working on multi-language codebases need fast, safe, structural code intelligence. The existing LSP MCP server provides semantic intelligence but is heavy and not always available. AST MCP fills the structural gap with a read-only, scope-bounded, language-server-free service.

text: Ship a Rust stdio MCP server exposing exactly 12 ast_* tools.
Support TypeScript, TSX, JavaScript, JSX, and Python at the parser level.
Enforce workspace-relative paths, bounded output, and a 1 MiB per-file cap.
Provide syntax-aware chunking and bounded Tree-sitter queries for agent workflows.
Remain usable when LSP MCP is unavailable; never call any lsp_* tool, language server, or semantic engine.
ast_health_check returns workspace status and parser availability for all 5 V1 languages.
ast_list_supported_languages returns the full registry with extension routing.
ast_parse_file parses valid .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx, and .py files and reports hasSyntaxError.
ast_file_outline returns classes, functions, imports, exports, types, and enums for TS/JS and Python.
ast_top_level_nodes returns direct root children in source order with optional text.
ast_enclosing_node finds the smallest node at a position with kind filter and ancestors.
ast_find_imports detects ES imports, CommonJS require, dynamic import, and Python imports.
ast_find_exports detects TS/JS exports and Python __all__ plus best-effort public definitions.
ast_find_functions and ast_find_classes return parameters, return types, methods, and parent class.
ast_chunk_file supports 4 strategies with stable chunk IDs and respects line/byte limits.
ast_query compiles and runs bounded Tree-sitter queries with structured errors on invalid input.
Paths outside the workspace are rejected; output is bounded; no tool writes files.
12 ast_* tools, 5 V1 languages, Tree-sitter parsing, single-workspace, bounded output.
WORKSPACE_PATH-based single-root workspace model with path safety and file size limits.
UTF-16 to byte-offset conversion helpers with non-BMP tests.
Language-specific extractors for imports, exports, functions, classes, and 4 chunking strategies.
Unit tests, integration tests, safety tests, and architectural lint tests.
LSP integration, semantic references, type resolution, compiler diagnostics.
Workspace-wide indexing, workspace-wide AST queries.
Framework-aware extraction (routes, React components, hooks, tests, decorators, schemas).
AST rewrites, file mutation, persistent state, caching (deferred to V5).
Remote workspace support, multi-root workspace support.
V2-V5 tool surface (deferred per spec section 36).
Composite workflows (code_context_pack, code_inspect_symbol, etc.).
Read-only: no fs::write, no tokio::fs::write, no OpenOptions::write, no fs::rename. Enforced by tests/architecture/no_write.rs.
No LSP: no lsp_* crate in Cargo.toml, no use lsp_ in src/. Enforced by tests/architecture/no_lsp.rs.
Single workspace root per process; supplied via WORKSPACE_PATH env var or CWD.
Public API uses UTF-16 character positions; conversion helpers required.

Default limits as V1 constants: maxFileBytes=1MiB, maxNodes=500, maxResults=200, maxTextBytes=20000, maxChunkLines=120, maxChunkBytes=30000, maxQueryMatches=200, parseTimeoutMs=5000, queryTimeoutMs=5000.
All responses are single JSON; errors are structured AstToolError. No prose in tool payloads.
Tool names match spec section 4 exactly. No additions, no removals.
Implementation lives at the project root /Volumes/Workspace/rnd/workflow/mcp/ast/; the Rust crate is initialized as a sub-crate alongside spec/, docs/, and .agent/.

Tree-sitter and language crate versions are pinned to the spec's recommendations: tree-sitter 0.22, tree-sitter-typescript 0.20, tree-sitter-javascript 0.20, tree-sitter-python 0.20. May pin to closest stable versions if these have moved.
MCP transport is either an existing Rust SDK or a thin JSON-RPC over stdio behind mcp::transport.
UTF-16 conversion is exact for ASCII, Latin-1, BMP, and surrogate pairs; full non-BMP correctness is asserted by tests but not formally proven.
Tree-sitter parses a 1 MiB file in well under 1 s on commodity hardware; the 5 s parseTimeoutMs is a safety net.
Test fixtures live in tests/fixtures/ and are committed, including a small corpus for each V1 language plus UTF-16 and syntax-error cases.
V1 scope is exactly the 12 tools named in spec/version-1.md section 4.
reason: Spec is unambiguous; tool surface must match exactly for downstream orchestration.

text: Rust plus Tree-sitter as the parser layer.
reason: Recommended by the spec; matches agent-server norms; Tree-sitter is the de facto standard for syntax trees across all 5 V1 languages.

text: Single workspace root per process.
reason: Spec section 7. Multi-root is a V5 concern.

text: Read-only server, no caches in V1.
reason: Spec sections 8.2 and 5. Caching is a V5 concern.

text: Public positions are UTF-16.
reason: Spec section 9. Aligns with LSP contract for future orchestration.

text: Bounded output is mandatory, not optional.
reason: Spec section 8.3. AST output can be huge; unbounded responses can OOM the client.

text: Errors are structured JSON, never prose.
reason: Spec sections 12 and 28. Tools are consumed by agents, not humans.

text: Internal architecture follows spec section 29: mcp/, config/, safety/, parser/, languages/, extractors/, tools/, shared/, utils/.
reason: Spec lists this layout; cleanly separates transport, parsing, and tool handlers.

text: The 12 development milestones become the phase plan.
reason: Spec section 33 enumerates 12 milestones, each a small, testable, committable unit.

text: V2-V5 are deferred, not pre-built.
reason: Spec section 36. V1 design must keep extension points open, but the implementation must not pre-build V2 capabilities.

text: Architectural lint tests enforce no-write and no-LSP.
reason: Constraints c-1 and c-2 are the most important invariants in the contract. Manual review is error-prone.

text: The 4 chunking strategies ship in one phase.
reason: They share the same chunk-iteration core; splitting them across phases adds coordination cost without benefit.

text: UTF-16 position conversion is subtly wrong for non-BMP characters.
Tree-sitter version drift breaks extraction.
Bounded output drops important context.
Never-write-files is broken by a future refactor.
Never-call-LSP is broken by a future refactor.
Workspace path traversal via symbolic links.
Query timeout is a soft limit, not a hard cancel.
Tree-sitter crashes on malformed input.
Chunk IDs are not stable across versions.
Multi-version spec drift.
JSON payload size for full parse trees.
